meta.title: ASF Battery Sector Comparison: Amara Raja vs Exide vs HBL Engineering
meta.data_coverage: FY25 (April 2024 – March 2025) with available FY26 actuals
meta.methodology: Adaptive Systems Framework (ASF) — public data only
meta.disclaimer: This analysis is based only on publicly available information and is not investment advice.
meta.extraction_status: Manual — all evidence human-verified from primary sources
meta.confidence_overall: HIGH for financials; MEDIUM for market share and segment splits

amara_raja.name: Amara Raja Energy & Mobility Ltd

lithium_ion_strategy.technology_partner: Gotion High-Tech (China) via GIB EnergyX Slovakia
lithium_ion_strategy.partnership_type: Technology licensing — LFP cells (cylindrical and prismatic)
lithium_ion_strategy.initial_chemistry: NMC 2170 cylindrical (Phase 1); LFP (Phase 2 via Gotion license)
lithium_ion_strategy.source: Company press releases, Autocar Professional Aug 2025

industrial_strength.market_position_claim: Pioneer in VRLA batteries in India; largest VRLA exporter (self-reported)
industrial_strength.railway_defense_revenue: Not separately disclosed; subsumed in industrial segment

key_risks: 96% revenue from lead-acid batteries (structural decline risk)
Chinese technology dependency (Gotion LFP, unnamed NMC partner)

score_rationale.strategic_clarity: Clear 'Amara Raja 3.0' new energy strategy with specific milestones; execution delay tempers score
score_rationale.capability_creation: Partnerships signed, CQP groundbreaking done, R&D lab underway; but no cell production yet
score_rationale.technology_transition_readiness: LFP license secured (Gotion), NMC co-development underway; timeline risk from gigafactory delay
score_rationale.manufacturing_readiness: 1.5 GWh pack plant operational; CQP nearing completion; gigafactory cell production FY27
score_rationale.execution_velocity: OEM MoUs signed quickly; however, gigafactory delayed 12-18 months
score_rationale.dependency_risk: High: 96% LAB revenue, Chinese tech licensor, antimony cost exposure
score_rationale.adaptation_gap: Significant gap between current (lead-acid incumbent) and required (Li-ion producer) state
score_rationale.crt_months_estimate: ~27 months to first commercial cell production (end FY27)
score_rationale.evidence_confidence: Strong public disclosures, CRISIL AA+, multiple broker reports
score_rationale.ceo_action_priority: Focus: gigafactory timeline discipline, margin recovery, antimony risk mitigation

exide.name: Exide Industries Ltd

financials_fy25.source: Q4 FY25 earnings call, ICRA rating rationale, multiple broker reports

lithium_ion_strategy.partnership_type: Technology licensing + turnkey plant setup assistance
lithium_ion_strategy.cell_plant_status: Commissioning/trial production (FY25 end); commercial target FY26-end
lithium_ion_strategy.anchor_ev_customer: Hyundai Motor Company + Kia Corporation (MoU, April 2024, LFP cells for India EVs)
lithium_ion_strategy.oe_pipeline: Two unnamed leading 2W OEMs in advanced discussions (NMC cylindrical)
lithium_ion_strategy.chemistry: NMC (3 GWh) + LFP (3 GWh) in Phase 1
lithium_ion_strategy.source: Hyundai global newsroom, Autocar Professional, ICRA rating, Benchmark Minerals

industrial_strength.lead_acid_market_position: Estimated ~50% organised automotive market share; 'dominant' per ICRA
industrial_strength.defense: Submarine batteries for Indian Navy; indigenous Type-IV class submarine battery development
industrial_strength.railway: Described as largest supplier to Indian Railways for signalling and emergency lighting batteries
industrial_strength.home_ups: Consumer inverter batteries — strong brand equity under Exide and SF brands
industrial_strength.recycling: 4 lead recycling plants (via Chloride Metals subsidiary) — significant backward integration
industrial_strength.confidence: MEDIUM for market share; HIGH for submarine and solar facts

key_risks: ₹5,000 crore Li-ion capex with zero revenue to date
Antimony prices surged 5x YoY — major margin headwind
Li-ion commercialization timeline repeatedly pushed (FY25 → FY26 → sample-only FY27)
Long-term lead-acid structural decline as EVs penetrate

score_rationale.strategic_clarity: Clear dual-track: protect and grow LAB; build Li-ion at scale. EESL as separate entity provides organizational clarity
score_rationale.capability_creation: 6 GWh gigafactory in commissioning; pack plant operational; Hyundai anchor secured
score_rationale.technology_transition_readiness: Further along than Amara Raja (plant commissioning vs still building); Hyundai/Kia anchor customer provides demand certainty
score_rationale.manufacturing_readiness: Best manufacturing readiness among three: 10 LAB plants + 4 recycling + 1.5 GWh pack + 6 GWh cell in commissioning
score_rationale.execution_velocity: Consistent margin improvement in LAB; Li-ion timeline has slipped but plant is physically built
score_rationale.dependency_risk: High due to SVOLT dependency, antimony exposure; partially offset by 4 recycling plants for lead
score_rationale.adaptation_gap: Smaller gap than Amara Raja — more advanced on Li-ion cell manufacturing; Hyundai anchor reduces demand risk
score_rationale.crt_months_estimate: ~18 months to first commercial Li-ion cell sales (FY27 sample deliveries, FY28 volume)
score_rationale.evidence_confidence: ICRA AAA, Hyundai newsroom, earnings call transcripts — very strong sourcing
score_rationale.ceo_action_priority: Focus: antimony cost mitigation, SVOLT dependency hedge, Li-ion ramp timeline execution

hbl.name: HBL Engineering Ltd

financials_fy25.source: CARE Ratings press release February 2026 (audited FY25 consolidated)

kavach_orderbook.source: CARE Ratings, Rail Analysis India, Business Standard

battery_segments.global_nicad_position: World's 2nd largest industrial Ni-Cad battery manufacturer (self-reported/analyst cited)
battery_segments.confidence: HIGH for Kavach; MEDIUM for defense claims

key_risks: High government/railway concentration — RDSO approval risk demonstrated in FY25
Telecom battery structural decline (migrating to Li-ion)
Order cancellation risk (24% of CLW order cancelled)
Working capital intensity (147 days in FY25 vs 114 days in FY24)
Ni-Cad at 90% capacity utilization — growth constrained without expansion

score_rationale.strategic_clarity: Very clear: Kavach + specialty/defense batteries + export expansion. Not trying to be a mass EV player — disciplined focus
score_rationale.capability_creation: First Kavach V4.0 certification; Ni-Cad near capacity; Li-ion growing organically; Tonbo Imaging stake for defence optics
score_rationale.technology_transition_readiness: Strong in niche chemistry (Ni-Cad, Silver-Zinc, Li-ion for defence/industrial); NOT positioned for mass EV Li-ion
score_rationale.manufacturing_readiness: 5 plants; Ni-Cad at 90% utilization (positive for pricing, constraint for growth); LAB underutilized (opportunity)
score_rationale.execution_velocity: First V4.0 certified, delivered 76% of CLW order on time; 40% export growth; 9M FY26 revenue up 80% YoY
score_rationale.dependency_risk: Highest dependency risk of three due to government/railway concentration; RDSO timeline risk demonstrated
score_rationale.adaptation_gap: Smallest gap — strategy fits current capability; not chasing a large technology leap for mass market
score_rationale.crt_months_estimate: ~9 months to Kavach full ramp (executing now); Li-ion defence/industrial expansion ~18 months
score_rationale.evidence_confidence: CARE Ratings audited figures are primary source — highest evidence quality of three companies
score_rationale.ceo_action_priority: Focus: Ni-Cad capacity expansion, railway concentration diversification, working capital reduction

comparison.strongest_financials_fy25: Exide Industries (highest revenue, ICRA AAA, zero net debt)

adaptation_gap_matrix.note: Scores out of 10; higher = more capable/ready in that dimension. Government dependency is inverted (lower score = higher dependency risk).

evidence_log.fact: Gotion LFP technology license signed June 24, 2024
Gigafactory Phase 1 delayed to FY27 end
Ather Energy agreement for NMC/LFP cell supply, August 2024

evidence_log.source: Q4 FY25 earnings call, multiple brokers

evidence_log.fact: Hyundai Motor + Kia MOU for LFP EV battery localisation, April 2024
EESL Bengaluru gigafactory: 6 GWh Phase 1, SVOLT technology
ICRA AAA/Stable rating; downgrade trigger: sustained Li-ion capex weakening debt metrics
Cumulative EESL investment ₹4,802 crore as of mid-2025
FY25 consolidated revenue ₹1,977.59 crore; EBITDA margin 20.35%; PAT ₹276.48 crore
CLW Kavach order ₹1,522 crore (December 2024); 76% executed by December 2025
First OEM to receive Kavach V4.0 RDSO certification, May 13, 2025
9M FY26 revenue ₹2,698.71 crore (+80% YoY); EBITDA margin 37.23%
